We hereby recommended that Management direct the Municipal
Treasurer or concerned official to:

a) Carry out at all times control procedures on proper handling of cash
collections and deposits.

b) Undertake regular updates of cashbooks and made available to COA anytime
for the necessary verification and examination.

c) Remit and deposit daily all collections or if collections reached the amount of
₱5,000.00, to avoid accumulation of the same in the custody of the collecting
officers and/or Municipal Treasurers, and avoid possible loss or
misappropriation thereof.

d) Undertake regular updates of cash accounts Subsidiary Ledgers (SLs) for easy
reconciliation of the cash balances with those records maintained by the
Municipal Treasurer.

e) Take note of the prescribed deadlines for submission of reports such as Bank
Reconciliation Statements (BRS), Bank Statements (BS), Report of
Collections and Deposits (RCD), Report of Accountability for Accountable
Forms (RAAF) and other reports duly supported with required documents.

f) Issue AF in chronological manner, and ensures that details in the Monthly
RAAF (ending balance) tallies with the actual accountable forms on hand.

We recommended that Management:

a) Instruct the inventory committee to prioritize and take charge the overall
conduct of physical count of PPE to prove the existence of the recorded
agency’s properties and require them to prepare the corresponding Report on
Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) with appropriate
remarks on the condition and whereabouts of the PPE.

b) Direct the accounting and property offices to maintain and update complete
PPELC and PC, respectively, per class of PPE to facilitate the reconciliation
of balances with the results of inventory taking and copy furnish the office of
the auditor with accurate descriptions and details not later than January 31 of
each year.

c) Invite representative from COA to act as witness in every physical count,
and the letter-request shall be served and stamped received by COA at least three
days prior to the scheduled date.

d) Report all obsolete and unserviceable PPEs in the Inventory and Inspection
Report on Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) duly signed by the head of the
accounting unit, inspection /appraisal committee and approved by the head of
the agency or authorized representative, and submit the report to COA
together with other required documents for the necessary inspection and
validation.

e) Disclose in the notes to FS the carrying amounts of PPE retired from active
use and held for disposal so as not to misled users of the FS, and upon
disposal, draw a JEV to derecognize the unserviceable properties from the
accounting books and property records.

f) Direct property officer to facilitate issuance and/or renewal of Property
Acknowledgement Report (PAR) every three years to ensure accountability
over government properties.

a) Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the required certified list
of taxpayers with the amount of taxes due and collectible, and submit the same to

b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to adjust the amount of

amount that is actually collectible for each taxpayer, if the required certified
list of taxpayers is already made available.

a) Require concerned officials to be more careful in its budget preparation
particularly on income estimation.

in budget planning.

strategies as required in the afore-mentioned laws and regulations.

We hereby recommended that the LCE undertake the following:

a. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to impose internal rules in the
movement or flow of transactions, like the claiming of checks, in order
to expedite submission of reports and documents to the Municipal
Accountant for submission of the latter to the auditor.

b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to transmit immediately all un-

communicated to management for its immediate and appropriation
action.

c. Comply with the required timeframe on the rendition of accounts
including preparation and submission of financial reports and so

a) Prepare and issue policy guidelines to address the issues on the necessity

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to management the following:
- The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat and the agency’s
Technical Working Group (TWG) shall adhere to R.A 9184 and to other
procurement rules and regulations in all their procurement undertakings.
- Submit all lacking documentation to enable us complete our audit on the said
fund and further undertake the required ocular inspection. Moreover, refrain
from processing claims/payments not duly supported with complete
documentation so as to avoid audit suspension and disallowance.
- Observe deadlines in reports submission for a timely audit and immediate
inclusion of its results in the Annual Audit report.

We recommended to management the following:
- The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat and the agency’s
Technical Working Group (TWG) shall adhere to R.A 9184 and to other
procurement rules and regulations in the procurement of LDRRM related
projects, programs and activities.
- The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer shall discontinue
processing of claims/payments for LDRRM related projects and activities
without appropriate documentation so as to avoid audit suspension and
disallowance.
- Concerned officials shall submit all lacking documentation including un-
submitted Disbursement Vouchers to enable us complete our audit on the
said fund.

- Fast track finalization of the Solid Waste Management (SWM) Plan and
have it approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission to
ensure that programs, projects and activities included in the plan are in
accordance with the National Solid Waste Management Framework.
- Complete other required documentation/data on Solid Waste Management
and submit copy to COA immediately thereafter.
- Ensure that proper mechanisms, like budget and manpower, are in place to
give assurance to proper implementation of the plan.
- Solid waste management programs/projects must align with the approved
SWM Plan. Any deviation from the said plan could be a ground for
disallowance in audit.
- Fully comply with the Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 and other
regulations pertaining thereto to be able to have a systematic and
comprehensive ecological waste management program.

We recommended that:
- The management shall always formulate GAD Plan with great emphasis
on proper planning and compliance to various GAD policies and
regulations.
- Maximize implementation of the budgeted amount for GAD related
programs and projects to be able to meet its 5% minimum requirement.
- Submit immediately all GAD related expenditures as well as settle audit
suspensions to enable us complete our audit on GAD Fund.

We hereby recommended the following measures:
- The Municipal Treasurer shall coordinate with the Municipal Assessor in
the preparation of the required Certified List of Taxpayers and immediately
provide copy of the same to the Municipal Accountant to be able to establish
accurate amount of basic RPT and SET receivables.
- Upon receipt of the latest Certified List of Taxpayers, the Municipal
Accountant shall make necessary adjusting entries to correct the recorded
amount of RPT/SET Receivable vis a vis the submitted list of taxpayers.
- The accounting office shall likewise maintain an index card for each
taxpayer to facilitate the establishment of the accurate amount of
collectibles from each taxpayer.
- Regularly update RPT/SET Receivable accounts Subsidiary Ledgers.

We recommended to management to enforce collection strategies and remedies
to increase RPT/SET collections as well as undertake measures to require all
delinquent taxpayers to prioritize payment of tax on real property within
deadlines and in accordance with existing regulations.

We recommended the following:
- Discontinue payment of claims not supported with complete documentation.
- Observe correct disbursement system.
- Exert all possible efforts to settle all audit suspensions within the required
timeframe to avoid disallowance in audit.
- Assign additional qualified personnel to take charge in the settlement of all
audit suspensions to be able to meet the required deadlines.

We hereby recommended to management the following:
- Head of Agency shall request and/or demand in writing from the Municipal
Treasurer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions, and
submit the same together with other supporting documents to the Accounting
Section within the time prescribed by the regulation.
- Head of the accounting section shall prioritize submission of transaction
documents and other financial reports to COA and maximize work
assignment among accounting personnel so as to prevent work overload and
meet deadlines on reports submission.
- Comply with the prescribed timeframe on the submission of accounts and
financial reports to avoid sanctions that may be imposed from violating
thereof as required under section 127 of P.D. 1445, which provides that:
Administrative disciplinary actions:
Subject to rules and regulations as may be approved by the president,
any unjustified failure by the public officer concerned to comply with
any requirement imposed in this Code shall constitute neglect of duty
and shall be a ground for administrative disciplinary action against
the said public officer who, with the degree of his guilt in accordance
with the civil service law. Repeated unjustified failure to comply with
the requirements impose in this Code shall be conclusive proof the
public officer concerned is notoriously undesirable.

We hereby recommended the following:
- Concerned offices shall give extra effort and prepare individual PPMPs
correctly and within the deadline.
- All procurement requirements shall be included in the prepared PPMPs
including those needed in the construction/repair of agency’s programs and
projects, and in various GAD and LDRRM related undertakings.
- PPMPs shall be prepared annually and submit the same to the Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC) immediately thereafter for consolidation into
Annual Procurement Plan (APP) using the prescribed format. This approved
APP shall serve as the control in any agency’s procurement; hence, no
procurement shall be undertaken unless it is in accordance with the said plan.

We hereby recommended the following:
- Budgeted amount intended for the above programs and activities shall be
implemented vis a vis its approved plans. Any deviation from the said plans
could be a ground for disallowance in audit.
- Concerned official shall submit immediately all the required documentation
relative to its proper implementation together with the breakdown of its
actual expenditures and other necessary data, if there is any, to facilitate the
conduct of our post-audit activity.

We hereby recommended that Bids and Awards Committee shall undertake
the following:
- Comply with the provisions stated in the procurement law specifically on
section 13.1 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184, to enhance transparency in the
procurement process; and likewise make it a valid procurement.

been sent to observers.

qualifications and possesses the required expertise relevant to goods/services

sanction that may be imposed from violating thereof.

LDRRM Fund following the required procedures and format.

LDRRM Registries of Appropriation, Allotments and Obligations for QRF,

records for LDRRM and make it available to COA for the necessary review

Statements.

Municipal Budget Officer shall always takes heed to the requirement of the law
and ensure that budget records were maintained and updated regularly to be
able to provide reliable information towards fair presentation of agency’s
Financial Statements.

- Prepare the required AAPSI within the deadline set, which is within 60 days
upon receipt of the Annual Audit Report, following the correct format and

Representatives, House Committee on Appropriations and Senate Committee

- Posting of the said report in the agency’s website is likewise enjoined.

accountabilities.

Administrative disciplinary actions:
Subject to rules and regulations as may be approved by the president,
any unjustified failure by the public officer concerned to comply with
any requirement imposed in this Code shall constitute neglect of duty
and shall be a ground for administrative disciplinary action against
the said public officer who, with the degree of his guilt in accordance
with the civil service law. Repeated unjustified failure to comply with
the requirements impose in this Code shall be conclusive proof that
public officer concerned is notoriously undesirable.

- The Municipal Mayor shall require the Municipal Engineer and all

observations.recommendation:
Disbursement Vouchers on the grant of cash advance shall bear specific legal purpose for which the cash advance was availed of to prevent possible disallowance in audit.
Stop the grant of cash advance on account of infrastructure or undertakings on project basis unless circumstances warrant its application.

Make necessary journal entries to record all parcels of land, as per reported list of land owned, under Land Account – General Fund books following the prescribed valuation in PPSAS 17 to be able to correct understatement of the PPE Account balance in the Financial Statements. However, identify those lots recorded in the books without the required legal documents of ownership as those should be properly disclosed in the notes to Financial Statements.
The management shall create a committee to fast track titling of the aforementioned parcels of land and/or secure any proof to establish real ownership and so the interest of the municipality on the properties is secured, and likewise ensure the legitimacy of recording in the books.
Appropriate enough funds for the titling or securing proof of ownership of these lands.

The management shall adhere to the aforementioned regulation as to frequency of the conduct of Physical Count on PPE including report preparation and submission, which has to be undertaken annually and submission thereof shall be made on or before January 31 of the ensuing year.
Invite representative from the Commission on Audit (COA) to act as witness in every inventory-taking to comply with the regulation. Invitation letter shall be served and stamped received by COA at least three days prior to the scheduled date to give the auditor an ample time to prepare and settle whatever time inconveniences that may occur.

The Municipal Budget Officer shall take heed to the rule of law and prioritize preparation of budget registries in 2018 using the required forms and update the same regularly to comply with the regulation.

We recommended that the municipality shall maintain special accounts in the general fund specifically for the 20% Development Fund through preparation and maintenance of complete subsidiary ledgers to comply with the afore-mentioned regulations as well as facilitate monitoring and easy preparation of  FS for special accounts.
Moreover, Municipal Accountant should prepare FS for special accounts and shall submit the same to COA as an attachment to the FS for General Fund.

Agency’s books of accounts which comprise complete copy of Journals and Ledgers shall be maintained using the prescribed forms, and update the same regularly and make it available anytime for COAs validation and verification.
Ensure that balances in the Financial Statements must have represent balances of these journals and ledgers as tallied with approved budget and actual expenditures.

The management shall always formulate GAD Plan with great consideration on proper planning and compliance to various GAD policies and regulations. Moreover, it should be viable and effective, and a catalyst in the attainment of its desired goals in promoting positive change in the lives of every constituent.
Assigned qualified personnel to handle GAD related undertaking so as to ensure effective and efficient implementation.
Maximize implementation of the budgeted amount for GAD related programs and projects to be able to meet its 5% minimum requirement.
Submit immediately all GAD related expenditures as well as settle audit suspensions to enable us complete our audit on GAD Fund.

The management shall conduct project/activity assessment and determine possible causes for non-implementation, and adopt necessary and effective measures to address the problem/s.
Likewise, formulate LDRRM Plan that is viable and effective, and will help achieve its objective that is to promote disaster risk reduction and management system within the municipality.

Instruction shall be given to concerned officials to complete the required documentation for 20% Development Fund and submit the same to COA immediately thereafter and so technical documentation can be forwarded to COA inspectorate team for the necessary inspection and validation.
Discontinue processing of claims and payments for 20% Development Funds without the required pre-implementation requirements and other required documents so as to avoid audit suspension and disallowance.

To ensure that effective check and balance exists, key duties and functions should be assigned to separate individuals. Hire/Designate personnel who possess the required skills and expertise to do the job efficiently. Send them to training for proper orientation and skills development, if necessary.  

Accounting section should require payee to submit the required   documentation before payments are being made.

Instruct officials concerned to submit the disbursement vouchers and other financial reports promptly, to meet the deadline set by the Commission on Audit which is every 15th day of the ensuing month. 

Discontinue the practice of processing claims/payments without proper and complete documentation.

Strictly observe the correct disbursement system.

The management shall formulate procurement process in accordance with the required procurement laws and regulations and with great consideration on proper procurement planning.

Ensure that all required documentation was attached to the Disbursement Vouchers/liquidation documents as it will facilitate completion of audit on procurement.

The approved Annual Procurement Plan (APP) shall be used as basis in the procurement of goods (office supplies, office equipment and construction materials) thru procurement service or competitive bidding. Items not included in the APP shall not be procured to comply with the regulations. However, APP could be revised or updated whenever necessary to suit the needs of the agency.

The management shall prioritize registration with PhilGEPS not only to take advantage of its built-in efficiencies but also avail of volume discounts inherent in bulk purchasing. It may also promote transparency and efficiency in the procurement process and give all interested parties an equal opportunity to bid.

The municipal treasurer shall make sure that supporting documents are present during check preparation and likewise, exercise extra care in its preparation to avoid errors and wastage of agency’s accountable forms.

Moreover, attached to the submitted Report of Checks Issued (RCI) all cancelled checks as it will facilitate the conduct of post-audit activity.

Facilitate immediate submission of appointments that are in questioned to avoid negative repercussion, as payment of salary without proper and valid appointment is considered irregular, hence, should be disallowed in audit.

Stop the practice of splitting requisition and purchase order. Items needed for one quarter and/or single activity/project shall be consolidated and procured in one purchase order.

observations.recommendation:
We hereby recommended the following:
Disbursement Vouchers on the grant of cash advances shall bear specific legal purpose for which the cash advance was availed of to prevent possible disallowance in audit and be free from sanction that may be imposed thereof as required under section 128 of P.D 1445, which states that:
Any violation of the provisions of section 67,68,89,106,108 of this Code or any regulation issued by the Commission implementing these sections, shall be punish by a fine not exceeding one thousand pesos or by imprisonment not exceeding (6) months, or both such find and imprisonment in the discretion of the court.
Cash advance shall not be used to pay reimbursements.
Prepare Report of Disbursement (ROD) per cash advance to enable us identify specific expenses paid out of that particular cash advance.

Facilitate immediate submission of appointments that are in questioned to avoid negative repercussion, as payment of salary without proper and valid appointment is considered irregular, hence, should be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that management shall undertake the following measures:
Limit the hiring of casual and contractual personnel based on the actual service necessity of the agency’s operation or whose job descriptions and functions are not in duplication and/or can be clearly defined in order to minimize expenditures for personal services.
Consider filling up of existing vacant positions instead hiring of casuals/contractual to perform the work pertaining to a vacant position.
Make revisions on the prepared Job Orders following the required format to comply with the above regulations.
Revisit existing polices on hiring if there are any; otherwise formulate the same to ensure compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations.

Recommendation:
The management shall undertake the following:
Review and adopt systematic procedures in the processing of claims/disbursements that will provide a check and balance of functions and accountabilities.
Direct all slow-performing officials to handle their assigned task with diligent and care as well as consider assigning additional personnel to accounting unit and to other key offices whose reports are significant in decision making, and so the reports can be prepared promptly and be able to meet deadlines on report submission, and be unconstrained from possible sanctions may be imposed from violating thereof  as required under section 127 of P.D. 1445, which provides that:
Administrative disciplinary actions:
Subject to rules and regulations as may be approved by the president, any unjustified failure by the public officer concerned to comply with any requirement imposed in this Code shall constitute neglect of duty and shall be a ground for administrative disciplinary action against the said public officer who, with the degree of his guilt in accordance with the civil service law. Repeated unjustified failure to comply with the requirements impose in this Code shall be conclusive proof the public officer concerned is notoriously undesirable.
Discontinue the practice of processing claims/payments without proper and complete documentation.
Strictly observe the correct disbursement system.

We have recommended that the management shall always invite representative from the Commission on Audit (COA) to act as witness in an inventory-taking to comply with the afore-cited regulation. Moreover, the said invitation shall be given at least three days prior to the scheduled date to give the auditor an ample time to prepare and settle whatever time inconveniences that may occur.

We hereby recommended the following:
Concerned officials shall give utmost importance to proper procurement planning where actual needs, right quantity and right processes, among others are considered.  Further, procurement above the threshold shall be done thru procurement service or public bidding to foster transparency and competitiveness and ensure the prices are the most advantageous to the municipality
To ensure continues supply, procurement shall not be done in staggered basis, and the agency shall make purchases in bulk and maintain buffer stocks good for one quarter consumption of items that are vital in their operation.
Stop the practice of splitting requisition and purchase order. Items needed for one quarter and/or single activity/project shall be consolidated and procured in one purchase order.
Discontinue the grant of cash advances other than travelling expenses to elected officials to comply with afore-cited regulations.
Include in CY 2017 action plan to be registered with PhilGeps to be able to fully utilize the PhilGeps and meets its objective on promoting transparency and efficiency in   procurement process and to give all interested parties an equal opportunity to bid.

Strict adherence to applicable law, rules and regulations on procurement is hereby enjoined.
Payment of pakyaw labor thru a supplier shall be stopped to avoid disallowance in audit.
Likewise, list of pakyaw group members shall be attached to every Pakyaw Contract.

Make necessary adjustment or revision on the submitted POW and Detailed Estimates to tally with the actual requirements of the project and with the deliveries made to prevent possible disallowance in audit.

The Municipal Budget Officer shall take heed to the rule of law and consider diligently the importance of maintaining the required Registries of Appropriation, Allotments and Obligations using the prescribed forms and update the same regularly.

Limit expenditures to what is appropriated in the budget to avoid incurrence of unauthorized expenditures.
Prepare and review carefully the budget so that actual needs of the Municipality are properly addressed and allocated.
Submit a duly approved ordinance/resolution authorizing such realignment and/or modification in the approved budget to avoid disallowance in audit.

As the grant of financial assistance to indigent families has always been one of the municipality’s major programs, we have recommended that the grant should be documented as follows:
-  Maintain a client logbook
-  Interview of client and submission of requirement to establish eligibility
Request for Financial Assistance duly approved by Mun. Mayor
Certificate of Eligibility as Indigent signed by MSWDO
Certificate of Death from Hospital or Barangay Captain for Burial Assistance
Medical Certificate from Hospital for Medical Assistance
Report from Bureau of Fire Protections or any authorized office for Fire Assistance
Schools statement of accounts for Educational Assistance
Acknowledgement Receipt
Likewise, a set of predetermine amount for each type of financial assistance should be established to institutionalize a non- discretionary determination of amount given to each client.

Books of Accounts shall be properly maintained and updated regularly and make it available anytime for validation and verification of COA representatives.
Ensure that balances in the Financial Statements must have represent balances in the approved annual budget as tallied with actual expenditures.
Moreover, the management shall send Municipal Accountant and person in-charge in accounting and bookkeeping functions to accounting related seminars to enhance their knowledge and skills in accounting and bookkeeping.

The Municipal Accountant shall make corrections and reflect actual agency assets in the PPE accounts under General Fund thru transfer of the total cost of completed projects and all equipment used in the construction from trust fund account and likewise closed those assets classified as public infrastructure to Registry of Public Infrastructure to comply with the requirement and to be able to have complete records of public infrastructure assets which will later become the basis in the implementation of the PPSAS and in the reclassification of infrastructure assets to PPE accounts following the required percentage.

The Municipal Accountant shall make necessary adjustments and properly record all tangible items covered by the afore-cited circulars to its appropriate accounts, by debiting appropriate Inventory Accounts (upon acquisition or  for unissued item)/expense accounts (for issued item)/prior years’ adjustments account (for prior year’s issuance) and crediting PPE accounts to correct and adjust balances of PPE accounts.
Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) shall be prepared by the issuing officer on every item issued to document the issuance of the item as well as monitor accountabilities.

JEVs preparation should always be based from source documents, which is the Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) and its supporting documents.  These DVs should likewise represent actual disbursements as tallied with the appropriation in the approved annual budget. Moreover, JEVs are used to record transactions from Journals to Ledgers to come up with the balances in the Financial Statements.
Make necessary adjustment and reclassification entries to correct deficiencies noted to ensure propriety and reliability of the Financial Statements.

We recommended that:
The municipality’s GAD Plan shall be formulated following proper planning with absolute consideration on various GAD policies and regulations, and shall align with programs, projects and activities in the Annual Investment Plan.
The 5% minimum requirement for GAD related programs and activities should be fully and properly utilized in accordance with GAD existing laws and regulations to ensure effective GAD implementation.
Data/information in the GAD accomplishment Report shall tally with actual disbursements to avoid confusion and doubts as to the true intention of the municipality for rendering unreliable information.

We have recommended that:
Discontinue the use of the 30% allocation for Quick Response unless supported with adequate supporting documents to avoid possible disallowance in audit.
A special trust fund account shall be maintained with an authorized depository bank for LDRRM sole purpose only and all unspent LDRRM Fund shall be immediately deposited thereto to comply with the afore-cited regulations.
Provide us copy of the Subsidiary Ledgers for Cash in Bank – LCAA account as well as duplicate copy of the deposit slip made to a LDRRM account to enable us completes our audit on LDRRMF.

Instruction shall be given to concerned officials to prepare the pre-implementation documents immediately and even before the start of project implementation to ensure meeting of deadlines, as well as avoid difficulties in its preparations as there were documents easily done and complied with less cost and effort if prepared prior to actual implementation.
Submit all the required documentation for inspection and validation immediately and within the deadline for a timely inclusion of its results in the Annual Audit report.

That the Bids and Awards Committee shall comply with the regulations on procurement through shopping. Procurement above the threshold should be done thru procurement service or public bidding pursuant to the provisions under RA 9184 to foster transparency and competitiveness and ensure the prices are the most advantageous to the municipality. However, splitting by dividing the projects into artificial phases to circumvent the regulations should not be resorted to.

Stop the payment of purchases out of the cash advance of the municipal treasurer and reimbursement basis unless circumstances warrant its application.

Required documents on procurement shall be complete, properly numbered, signed and dated.
observations.reason:
Economy and Expediency of procurement are among the major reasons why they failed to comply with the audit recommendations.
